source.title: The international EAACI/GA²LEN/EuroGuiDerm/APAAACI guideline for the definition, classification, diagnosis, and management of urticaria
source.population: People with acute, chronic spontaneous, or inducible urticaria.
source.setting: Allergy, dermatology, and general clinical assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for urticaria definition and classification; detailed management text was not accessible and is not mapped.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines urticaria as a mast-cell-driven disease presenting with wheals, angioedema, or both and distinguishes acute and chronic forms.

items.rationale: Exact phrase located in inspected full source.

source.title: Emergency treatment of anaphylaxis: Guidelines for healthcare providers
source.population: Adults and children with suspected anaphylaxis.
source.setting: Hospital and out-of-hospital healthcare settings.
source.exclusions: Used only to support anaphylaxis warning documentation associated with rash or urticaria; it does not convert isolated skin findings into anaphylaxis.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

items.final_wording: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

source.title: The international EAACI/GA²LEN/EuroGuiDerm/APAAACI guideline for the definition, classification, diagnosis, and management of urticaria
source.population: People with acute, chronic spontaneous, or inducible urticaria.
source.setting: Allergy, dermatology, and general clinical assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for urticaria definition and classification; detailed management text was not accessible and is not mapped.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Defines urticaria as a mast-cell-driven disease presenting with wheals, angioedema, or both and distinguishes acute and chronic forms.

items.rationale: Supports mapped urticaria, hives, wheals, angioedema, acute or chronic classification, and compatible observed skin or swelling fields.

items.final_wording: States that chronic spontaneous or inducible urticaria can impair quality of life and work or school performance and that the guideline covers classification, diagnosis, and management.

source.title: The international EAACI/GA²LEN/EuroGuiDerm/APAAACI guideline for the definition, classification, diagnosis, and management of urticaria
source.population: People with acute, chronic spontaneous, or inducible urticaria.
source.setting: Allergy, dermatology, and general clinical assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for urticaria definition and classification; detailed management text was not accessible and is not mapped.
source.evidence_paraphrase: States that chronic spontaneous or inducible urticaria can impair quality of life and work or school performance and that the guideline covers classification, diagnosis, and management.

items.rationale: Supports mapped acute versus chronic context and quality-of-life impact only; detailed management text was not accessible.

items.final_wording: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

source.title: Emergency treatment of anaphylaxis: Guidelines for healthcare providers
source.population: Adults and children with suspected anaphylaxis.
source.setting: Hospital and out-of-hospital healthcare settings.
source.exclusions: Used only to support anaphylaxis warning documentation associated with rash or urticaria; it does not convert isolated skin findings into anaphylaxis.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

items.rationale: Supports mapped breathing, lip or tongue swelling, dizziness or syncope, widespread swelling, mucosal, and known-trigger warnings while explicitly rejecting skin-only anaphylaxis inference.

items.final_wording: Supports documenting known allergen exposure, food or drug triggers, action-plan discussion, and specialist follow-up after suspected anaphylaxis.

source.population: People with acute, chronic spontaneous, or inducible urticaria.

added_items.rationale: Supports mapped urticaria, hives, wheals, angioedema, acute or chronic classification, and compatible observed skin or swelling fields.

added_items.final_wording: States that chronic spontaneous or inducible urticaria can impair quality of life and work or school performance and that the guideline covers classification, diagnosis, and management.

source.title: The international EAACI/GA²LEN/EuroGuiDerm/APAAACI guideline for the definition, classification, diagnosis, and management of urticaria
source.population: People with acute, chronic spontaneous, or inducible urticaria.
source.setting: Allergy, dermatology, and general clinical assessment.
source.exclusions: Exact for urticaria definition and classification; detailed management text was not accessible and is not mapped.
source.evidence_paraphrase: States that chronic spontaneous or inducible urticaria can impair quality of life and work or school performance and that the guideline covers classification, diagnosis, and management.

added_items.rationale: Supports mapped acute versus chronic context and quality-of-life impact only; detailed management text was not accessible.

added_items.final_wording: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

source.title: Emergency treatment of anaphylaxis: Guidelines for healthcare providers
source.population: Adults and children with suspected anaphylaxis.
source.setting: Hospital and out-of-hospital healthcare settings.
source.exclusions: Used only to support anaphylaxis warning documentation associated with rash or urticaria; it does not convert isolated skin findings into anaphylaxis.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Recognises rapid symptoms with airway, breathing, or circulation problems and usually skin or mucosal changes such as urticaria or angioedema; skin changes alone are not anaphylaxis.

added_items.rationale: Supports mapped breathing, lip or tongue swelling, dizziness or syncope, widespread swelling, mucosal, and known-trigger warnings while explicitly rejecting skin-only anaphylaxis inference.

added_items.final_wording: Supports documenting known allergen exposure, food or drug triggers, action-plan discussion, and specialist follow-up after suspected anaphylaxis.

source.title: Emergency treatment of anaphylaxis: Guidelines for healthcare providers
source.population: Adults and children with suspected anaphylaxis.
source.setting: Hospital and out-of-hospital healthcare settings.
source.exclusions: Used only to support anaphylaxis warning documentation associated with rash or urticaria; it does not convert isolated skin findings into anaphylaxis.
source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting known allergen exposure, food or drug triggers, action-plan discussion, and specialist follow-up after suspected anaphylaxis.

added_items.rationale: Supports mapped food or medicine trigger, action-plan, return, and clinician-arranged allergy follow-up documentation after suspected anaphylaxis.
